source.title: Allision Incident - M/V Adriatic strikes Marlin Platform

context.region: Gulf of America, Viosca Knoll Block 915

actions_taken: Manual Emergency Shut Down (ESD) of platform
Bleed down of platform by control room operators
Notification to USCG, NRC, and BSEE
Repairs to Dorado flowline and gas lift
event.summary: On 04 May 2025 at 0445 hours, motor vessel Adriatic (178-foot supply boat) struck the Marlin Tension Leg Platform at Viosca Knoll Block 915. The vessel's bow contacted the Northwest pontoon and stern contacted the Southwest pontoon, resulting in damage to production risers (Dorado and Crown and Anchor), internal gas lift line, and firewater piping. No injuries, environmental pollution, or stability issues were reported. The allision was caused by the vessel captain's inattention to navigation while performing administrative tasks and the absence of a second watch stander on the bridge.
recommendations: BSEE recommends the Office of Incident Investigation (OII) issue a Safety Alert on this incident
Safety Alert should be distributed to personnel working on boats/vessels that tend to facilities, not just facility operators
key_phrases: vessel underway without proper helm attention

threats.name: Vessel captain distracted from navigation by administrative tasks
Absence of second watch stander on bridge during vessel transit
Vessel approaching platform at speed without course correction

consequences.name: Structural damage to platform hull columns and risers
Damage to production flowlines (Dorado and Crown and Anchor)
Production shutdown and loss of revenue

controls.name: Bridge watch stander requirement during vessel transit

supporting_text: Typically, there is a 2nd Captain on the bridge at all times to ensure there is always somebody at the helm. In this case, the Captain had already granted permission to the 2nd Captain to leave the bridge
the requirement there will always be a minimum of one other watch stander in the bridge during periods when the vessel is underway

controls.name: Captain focus on vessel navigation during transit
controls.barrier_role: Maintain situational awareness and active helm control

supporting_text: the captain was not focused on captaining the vessel at the time of the incident. Instead, he was focusing on preparing paperwork for the upcoming drift test
the captain focused his attention on administrative tasks instead of navigation of the vessel
the captain wasn't aware of his surroundings at the time of the incident

controls.name: Drift test procedure and approval process
controls.barrier_role: Ensure controlled testing with proper oversight
controls.lod_basis: Operational procedure for vessel testing near platform

supporting_text: the M/V captain asked the CRO if he could perform a drift test. The CRO advised that the captain could perform his drift test as requested
either personnel thought the vessel was drifting, while it was actually underway, or the vessel wasn't performing the drift test at the time of the incident
The video of the allision clearly shows the vessel approaching the facility at speed

controls.name: 500-meter exclusion zone enforcement
controls.barrier_role: Maintain safe distance between vessel and platform

supporting_text: The CRO directed the M/V's captain to remain outside of the 500-meter zone until the Anadarko Balast Control Operator (BCO) was on tower
At 0445 hours, Marlin was struck by the M/V Adriatic

controls.name: Platform surveillance camera monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Detect approaching vessel and provide early warning

supporting_text: a video from a platform surveillance camera capturing the moments leading up to the allision, as well as when the M/V Adriatic allided with the platform was also provided to the investigators
The video of the allision clearly shows the vessel approaching the facility at speed

controls.name: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system activation
controls.barrier_role: Isolate production systems and prevent escalation

supporting_text: the allision impact was felt throughout the platform, which resulted in a manual Emergency Shut Down (ESD) and bleed down of the platform by nighttime control room operators (CRO)
the CRO ESD'd the platform. Personnel mustered while production personnel began shutting in wells, making the facility safe

controls.name: Firewater system isolation and repair
controls.barrier_role: Contain damage and restore safety systems

supporting_text: production personnel began shutting in wells, making the facility safe, and isolating a small section of firewater piping
The platform firewater piping has been repaired and returned to service as well

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
